summary: n8n-nodes-probe exfiltrates Kubernetes credentials and opens reverse shell to hardcoded C2 on load
details: The package's main entry is a top-level IIFE that executes on require(). It reads /home/ubuntu/.kube/config, runs kubectl recon commands (pods, secrets, deployments, nodes, ingress) using the stolen kubeconfig, collects host identifiers via `hostname` and `hostname -I`, and TCP-sends the aggregated JSON to hardcoded IP 103.27.109.184 on port 8890. The same IIFE spawns `/bin/bash -c` wiring bash stdio to a TCP socket at 103.27.109.184:8895, giving the remote endpoint an interactive shell on the installer's host. Both actions fire whenever n8n loads the package, with no user gating. The destination IP is unrelated to n8n or any legitimate n8n-nodes infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
